memory supply constraints and related pricing on demand from several handset customers

payload.management_tone_summary: Management strikes a bifurcated tone — celebrating record-level revenues and automotive momentum while simultaneously flagging a newly identified headwind in handset demand tied to industry-wide memory supply constraints. The explicit acknowledgment that Q2 FY2026 guidance embeds a memory-supply impact on 'several handset customers' introduces a more cautious near-term posture versus the prior quarter, even as long-term FY2029 goal rhetoric remains intact. The combination of record results with a forward caveat on the largest revenue segment (handsets) creates a mixed signal that leans slightly bearish on near-term estimates.
payload.analyst_revision_rationale: The explicit callout of 'memory supply constraints and related pricing' affecting 'several handset customers' in Q2 guidance is a new and specific risk disclosure that was not present in prior quarters, suggesting analysts may need to trim their near-term QCT handset revenue forecasts. Handsets represent the single largest QCT revenue stream, so even moderate demand softness from this factor could compress Q2 revenue and EPS below current consensus. Analysts may be underweighting the pricing dimension of the memory constraint (i.e., not just supply but also pricing pressure flowing through to Qualcomm's customers' demand decisions). The Alphawave acquisition adds some earnings dilution in the near term via acquisition-related items noted in diluted EPS guidance footnotes, which is another drag analysts may not have fully modeled. Collectively, these signals — new supply-chain headwind language, multi-customer impact, and acquisition dilution — point to modest downward revisions to Q2 FY2026 estimates, even as the longer-term AI and automotive growth narrative remains credible.
